Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Weekly pipeline has been failing for over a month. The client has been sending file as CAQH File.* instead of CAQH file.* (lowercase). Pipeline is manually rerun each week using Override Parameters to complete successfully

old

{"source_data_pattern": "CAQH file .*.xlsx", "source_entity_1_file_pattern": "CAQH file .*.xlsx"}

new

{"source_data_pattern": "CAQH File .*.xlsx", "source_entity_1_file_pattern": "CAQH File.*.xlsx"}

Suggestion: set source_data_pattern and source_entity_1_file_pattern to CAQH. * .xslx to account for both variations the client sends, or CAQH File..xlsx

[IT-3022274] Request for Data Validation: Claim Raw/model vs NILE Raw Data (Claims Header Fields) Created: 29/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Open
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Hagelin, Lindsay Assignee: Devatha, Satish
Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: None
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 30/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Client:  BCBS_ND

We are validating data in the ARK platform and observed that all fields in the Claims Header are populated from the Claim model, which in turn is sourced from Claim raw data. The values in the Claim model and raw data match exactly with the Claims Header, indicating that the data is loading as per the current logic. Since the Claim model data originates from NILE, we would like to understand how the raw data from NILE is structured and whether any transformations are applied to the relevant columns before being delivered downstream. Currently, we do not have visibility into the NILE raw files/tables to verify this.

For example members CH_PATIENT_CLIENT_MASTER_ID (member_id) IN (135557853, 66442765, 93553925), OPS has requested validation of the raw data to determine whether the client is sending multiple policy/UMI plans per member. The goal is to identify whether the root cause of the mapping issue is related to how the client sends the data or a Cotiviti mapping issue. The columns to validate in the Claims Header are [PatNum], [PatFirstName], [PatLastName], [EmployerName], and [GroupNum], with corresponding Claim Model/Raw columns CH_PATIENT_CLIENT_ID, CH_PATIENT_FIRST_NAME, CH_PATIENT_LAST_NAME, CH_GROUP_NAME, and CH_GROUP_NUMBER.

[IT-3020658] NAS Spoolers failed and pipeline not completed,Hue browser not working in production environment. Created: 29/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 29/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar Assignee: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: Internal
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 5 hours
Original Estimate: Not Specified

GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Summary: (Object(s) that caused the issue (host names, etc), issues/symptoms, include a link to the alert if there was one) - There are 20 missing blocks due to which Pipelines are unable to pick any jobs 
Resolution: (Actions taken by support teams to resolve the issue, Team/Individual that fixed the issue, additional tickets involved) - Restarted the Nile prod cluster (usapnlhdpe01)
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Actual Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Today, we experienced a Hadoop issue where spoolers failed, pipelines got stuck, and the Hue browser became inaccessible.

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

The HUE browser is not accessible, and from the Nile side all NAS spoolers have failed. The failure occurred while trying to write files to HDFS. The error indicates that the HDFS NameNode is in safe mode, which prevents any file creation or write operations.

Because of this, the spooler is unable to write files to the HDFS path, causing all spooling processes to fail. The system will remain in safe mode until the required block threshold is met, after which it should automatically turn off and resume normal operations.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hadoop noticed the issue and rolling restart the cluster. Instatly issue was resolved.
After sometime pipeline are not running as expected. we notify and connected with different teams.
They noticed the issue and resolved with the cluster restart.
Summary: (Object(s) that caused the issue (host names, etc), issues/symptoms, include a link to the alert if there was one) - There are 20 missing blocks due to which Pipelines are unable to pick any jobs 
Resolution: (Actions taken by support teams to resolve the issue, Team/Individual that fixed the issue, additional tickets involved) - Restarted the Nile prod cluster (usapnlhdpe01)

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

Summary: (Object(s) that caused the issue (host names, etc), issues/symptoms, include a link to the alert if there was one) - There are 20 missing blocks due to which Pipelines are unable to pick any jobs 
Resolution: (Actions taken by support teams to resolve the issue, Team/Individual that fixed the issue, additional tickets involved) - Restarted the Nile prod cluster (usapnlhdpe01)

Status: On Hold
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Giri, Kushal Assignee: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar
Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: RCA
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 1 hour
Original Estimate: Not Specified

GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting User Information
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hello, 

Currently, BCNAM is fully GWF eligible and everything on our end is configured this way. However, we are still seeing claims come through as not GWF eligible. We were wondering if this field is hardcoded at the GDF/CDF level. 

Can you please provide how these fields are currently mapped?

GDF: CH_US_ONLY_INDICATOR

CDF: US_ONLY_YN

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 05/May/26 EDT ]

Hi Giri, Kushal,
Good morning.
I have verified the mappings and provided the below information to here.
There is no raw field mapping for the below field in the GDF.

CAST(NULL AS STRING) AS CH_US_ONLY_INDICATOR

No mapping for the US_ONLY_YN

 

Comment by Jani, Shaik [ 05/May/26 EDT ]

Details have been conveyed to the customer. awaiting response.

Our IT will be making a change to the post-pay CLAIMS Extract.

Go Live – 5/28/2026

Change:

  • Addition of field at the end of the file
  • New field: Provider_Adjust_Amount – This field now contains the Medicare Sequestration amount on a claim line (net_amt field is unchanged, but now the sequestration amt is explicitly listed in this field)

 

Trey Simpson – AVP, Payment Integrity – Molina Healthcare, Inc.

[IT-3017571] NAS Spoolers failed in production environment.  Created: 28/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 29/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar Assignee: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: Internal
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 4 hours
Original Estimate: Not Specified

GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: We have delivered the files on time. connected with pc team get the confirmation from the user.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

we have noticed Nwe have noticed NAS Spoolers failed in production environment. 

Error: 

The job failed because our system tried to call another service to get file transfer logs, but that service didn’t respond in time.

File spooling failed due to a timeout while calling the external transfer logs API.

Error: 

The job failed because our system tried to call another service to get file transfer logs, but that service didn’t respond in time.

File spooling failed due to a timeout while calling the external transfer logs API.

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

I contacted the Command Center, and they will set up a bridge call with the Unix, Platform, and Axway teams.
Service desk ticket from command center https://jiraet.cotiviti.com/browse/IT-3014827

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

Issue :
Summary: (Object(s) that caused the issue (host names, etc), issues/symptoms, include a link to the alert if there was one)  Automatic Process of spooling files for PPM clients was affected. The Admin Portal for Transfer2 SFTP URL which is used for the for monitoring or tracking is hung on gad1pt2axcore02 server and caused the log monitoring API not responded. (The NAS spoolers are getting failed to fetch the file from NAS dropbox to Nile HDFS location.)
Resolution: (Actions taken by support teams to resolve the issue, Team/Individual that fixed the issue, additional tickets involved) MFT team has restarted the Admin Portal Services to restore the functionality and requested Nile team to use the FQDN url instead of single server url https://transfer2admin.cotiviti.com/api/v2.0/docs/index.html

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

We manually copied the files into ML_Nile B2B and placed them in the HDFS locations. We will monitor the pipelines to ensure the files are delivered on time.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

We have delivered the files on time. connected with pc team get the confirmation from the user.

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Judy,
Nile don't process ccv for BCBS AL;
We only ingest the raw files from the client to the staging tables, and from there, EDM RCA carry the downstream processes, including parts of ccv processes.
Let me check with Sajjan Shrestha to confirm;

Thx

melly

moving to Nile per engineer not for CRS Support

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 06/May/26 EDT ]

This is not with Nile - I'm not sure yet how we should proceed.

[IT-3011677] Please confirm if RCA Engineering is receiving and ingesting the SIU Weekly Provider Report Geisinger sends us via SFTP that is an exclusion file for our teams. Created: 24/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 01/May/26 EDT  Resolved: 01/May/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Depolo, Kara Assignee: Easley, Keaton
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: None
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: image-1.png     image.png    
Date Need By: 27/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Production Applications - RCA
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Provided the requestor's analysis in the comments which included comparing 4 SFTP files against files received from email
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Geisinger sends us a weekly SIU Provider exclusion file that should be received via SFTP. They also send it to us via e-mail. However, when CCV and I first began receiving this via e-mail, Phil Thomas, informed the CCV CSM and I we could ignore these e-mails as they get sent to us via SFTP and automatically ingested. It appears from the below that is the case for CCV. Do you know if that is still the case for RCA?

 

Recently the client, began adding additional exclusions to this list that we do have to share with Audit Ops for updates. This concerned me that perhaps this could impact our appropriate ingestion of the SIU weekly provider exclusions the client sends us via SFTP.

 

Would you mind having the team look into this for us and confirm if we are ingesting these files via the SFTP? If we are, are the files we’re ingesting, the same as the examples the client sends to us via e-mail as well?

Please let me know who I need to send a copy of the e-mail exclusion file to once this ticket is picked up. Thank you!!

 Comments   
Comment by Depolo, Kara [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Easley, Keaton [ 27/Apr/26 EDT ]

Active Pipelines

dm-geisinger-facets-weekly_SIU_notouch_file

geisinger-facets-weekly_provider_report_passthrough

File: SIU Weekly Provider Report.*

Filesource: /B2B/Geisinger/inbound/production

Cadence: Weekly

I compared SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 12-1-2025 file spooled from SFTP with SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 12-1-2025 file Reporter received via email. These files have many discrepancies, namely it seems the reports were generated at different times. Namely, the email file has a report date of 12/4/2025, while the file spooled from SFTP has a report date of 12/17/2025.

I compared SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 04-06-2026 file spooled from SFTP with SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 04-06-2026 file Reporter received via email. These files both contain 519 records. The files also contain 4,152 data points that match exactly. So these files are the same.

Comment by Easley, Keaton [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

 

SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 02-23-2026
Records 452
ValuesMatched 3616
ValuesUnmatched 0

 

SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 03-09-2026
Records 477
Values 3808
ValuesUnmatched 0

 

SIU Weekly Provider Report for Vendor 01-12-2026
Records 423
ValuesMatched 2965

Hello team

we received Cirrus weekly files on 4/13, would you please help reingest all the files?

This is a very time sensitive request, we will need your help to get the file successfully ingested today.

Thanks and please let me know if you need additional info.

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

The second set of files is not being spooled, and we’re facing several challenges. For example, the file name pattern does not include any suffix, and the files have the same name and size as those previously processed. When attempting to spool them, the system indicates that the files have already been processed.

I also tried placing all folders in the HDFS location, but the spooler got stuck, so I reached out to the platform team. As an alternative, we copied the files from FTP to HDFS with Satish’s help. The data is currently still loading.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

we successfully proceed the second set of files, verifying the tables data.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

Second set of files proceed from the Nile side.

Hi Nile PS Triage team,

Comment by Spinelli, Adam [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

  • prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260406_1.txt.pgp

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

waiting follow up from Adam.

Comment by Spinelli, Adam [ 30/Apr/26 EDT ]

Premera has sent over a new set of files. It appears that these files were successfully received in Axway. Can we please confirm they were loaded correctly?

  1. prebc_history_institutional_incoming_20260428_1.txt
  2. prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260428_1.txt

They also resent the unlinked file as well:

  1. prebc_unlinkedadj_incoming_1_20260428.zip
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 30/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Adam,
We are still getting an error on the replacement files 4/28.
We're taking a closer look...
Thank you

melly

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 30/Apr/26 EDT ]

Melly's update 4/30:
I can see both header-trailer in the source files:

/dataingestionprod/unrestricted/109-premera-bluecross/rawincoming/2026-03-30-91183910/1/136533/src_file_backup/20260428/1/prebc_history_institutional_incoming_20260428_1.txt

/dataingestionprod/unrestricted/109-premera-bluecross/rawincoming/2026-03-30-102438457/1/136533/src_file_backup/20260428/1/prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260428_1.txt

Pipeline error message:

No of columns not matching in Header record or Trailer record.

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 30/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Adam,
I got my answer:
The reason of the error is because they dont conform with the regular history trailer records because they dont have the date stamp at the end:
We received
@@TRAILER@@|428      (for institutional)

@@TRAILER@@|2002    (for professional).

Expected:

@@TRAILER@@|428|date time      (for institutional)

@@TRAILER@@|2002|date time    (for professional).

Would you mind asking the client for the replacement files?
Thanks so much

melly

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 04/May/26 EDT ]

Client finally sent their final replacement files and they were processed successfully:
prebc_history_institutional_incoming_20260503_1.txt.pgp
prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260503_1.txt.pgp

 Description   

I received the below question from Premera, and was hoping to get some help with the questions highlighted. Thanks!

 

We received an ack file last week (PREBC_appeals_ack_20260417085524) that contained Appealed Line Sequence of 79 characters, while prior guidance indicated a 50‑character maximum. Our system enforced the 50‑character limit, causing processing to fail.

We’ve temporarily increased our limit to 255, but could you please confirm:

·         Why this field exceeded 50 characters?

·         What is the expected maximum length going forward?

Attachments: carfh_history_incoming_1.07-08-2025_0530.txt     carfh_history_incoming_1.07-09-2025_0320.txt     Cotiviti Detail IDR_Feb26 Invoice_CLAIM_IDs.xlsx     _EXTERNAL_ RE_ Claims Hist Feed_Follow-up.msg     Failed Claim Lines ICMS - CSPRD6.xlsx    
Date Need By: 01/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other

Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

CareFirst is reporting that they have 800+ claims that we have invoiced for missing history. CareFirst claims to have sent the claims via History. I have run a few failed claims line reports and I do not see any reason why these would not have processed.

CareFirst has confirmed these are all Outpatient claims and the CLCL_Paid_DT field represents the date they sent us the History file. I have attached the report of all claims they were invoiced for as it relates to missing history. The goal of this inquiry is to ensure we received the files, which I am attaching two raw data files. And also if we did receive why did they not process and how can we track any possible file/line failures in the future?

The reason this is a concern is that CareFirst is billed for any unreturned history after 180 days. In order to avoid any unnecessary fees, CareFirst would like the opportunity to resolve any missing or failed history prior to the 180 days.

Original Email:

Subject: RE: Claims Hist Feed>Follow-up

From: Thachapilly, Abhilash Abhilash.Thachapilly@carefirst.com

To: Snyder, Amery amery.snyder@cotiviti.com

CC: Saubhagya.Sarangi@carefirst.com; McDonald, Christina Christina.McDonald@carefirst.com; Vinay.Kommera@carefirst.com; lauren.nunez Lauren.Nunez@carefirst.com; jenn.livengood Jenn.Livengood@carefirst.com; jeff.rose@carefirst.com Jeff.Rose@carefirst.com; Jefferson, Jaclyn Jaclyn.Jefferson@carefirst.com; Boddu, Srikanth Srikanth.Boddu@carefirst.com

Mon 4/13/2026 12:21 PM

Good morning Amery!

 Can you check with team to see why below claims were not loaded to coti history tables? There are almost 800+ claims Invoce team reported as missing paid feed. If Cotiviti runs an error report, we can identify the root cause of the fallouts

 Attached is the list of all claims reported as missing paid feed. But for an initial analysis below sample from Jul2025 are enough.

[IT-3004426] Humana PMDM File Ingestion Setup Created: 21/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 06/May/26 EDT

Status: In Progress (HD1)
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Franklin, Maria Assignee: Devatha, Satish
Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: rca_actian
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: image.png    
Date Need By: 30/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Creating ticket per Joy’s request.

What is the request? Ingest PMDM files into SQL tables,
What files do you need Nile to ingest and create spoolers and pipelines? What is the file name?

The PMDM extract is now two CSV files compressed into a single zip named PMDM_YYYYMM.zip. When you extract the zip file, you will see the two individual files as shown below.

  1. prov_ntwk_YYYYMM
  2. prov_pymt_ctrct_dly_YYYYMM

Please provide data dictionary in the ticket. The final updates to the ETL and Guide are being completed and will be sent to Cotiviti by EOW,

It seems Humana posted two file patterns for Cotiviti to work on. prov_pymt_ctrct_dly_* prov_ntwk_Historical_*

Are these two file patterns for Humana PMDM? How many kinds of files that we need to process for PMDM (because the dictionary said we should get PMDM_YYYYMM)?

1, This 1st FTP includes historical data thru March2026. The 4/9 email screenshot below details what is included in each file.

These files include data from the past 18 months, along with all related contracts from previous years that fall within that 18-month period.

2, Going forward with April2026 data next month the PMDM extract will be sent with the below naming convention (2 CSV files inside the zip file - PMDM_YYYYMM).

prov_ntwk_YYYYMM

prov_pymt_ctrct_dly_YYYYMM

Where do you need this data ingested to? What SQL server, database and table names?

  • Table = CnlyProvider
  • For the PMDM file, it has never been used by RCA. A new Table would need to be created (Need to confirm with Sajjan). 
  • Field Updates to be in Decipher
  • Screens to be updated: Claims Header/Claims Detail, & ACE

 
What date in the month that the PMDM file(s) will arrive? Sent monthly on the 5th
When will we expect to receive the monthly files? The files you just received has all PMDM data thru March2026. You will receive April2026 data on your regular schedule which will be the 5th of each month.
How would we receive the files? The PMDM file was FTP’d to Cotiviti on 4/7 and the just resent yesterday 4/20 per the client. Will they send to use? Do we need to download from their SFTP site? Yes, files will be delivered at the same location as other Humana files are received today.

Regular site  https://st.humana.com/ 

Non-production site  https://qa-st.humana.com/

 Comments   
Comment by Franklin, Maria [ 21/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Franklin, Maria [ 21/Apr/26 EDT ]

R2B2 Ticket reference [RCASA-74509] Humana - Enhance PMDM Monthly File Ingestion to Support Three New Specialty Fields - R&D Jira - WDC

Comment by Franklin, Maria [ 27/Apr/26 EDT ]

Updated Data Dictionary from client located here: Humana Supplemental Data Extract Guide_4.0

Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Prasad, Nirav

Data Copied
please verify

hdfs dfs -cp /dataingestionqc/unrestricted/18-uhc-cosmos/Processed/GDF/claims/medical/postcheck/202510_1 /dataingestiondev/unrestricted/18-uhc-cosmos/Processed/GDF/claims/medical/postcheck

hdfs dfs -chown -R svcddataunrestricted /dataingestiondev/unrestricted/18-uhc-cosmos/Processed/GDF/claims/medical/postcheck/
hdfs dfs -chgrp -R devunrestricted /dataingestiondev/unrestricted/18-uhc-cosmos/Processed/GDF/claims/medical/postcheck/
hdfs dfs -chmod -R 770 /dataingestiondev/unrestricted/18-uhc-cosmos/Processed/GDF/claims/medical/postcheck/

[IT-3004238] Healthspring DM 5381 - Claim Date Spans Showing on All Lines in Detail Decipher Screens Created: 21/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 05/May/26 EDT

Status: On Hold
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Price, Ryon Assignee: Kantimahanti, Ravisankar
Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: rca_actian
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: image-2026-03-05-10-55-36-354.png     image-2026-03-05-11-00-33-158.png    
Date Need By: 11/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Engineering
GSR Category:
HC RCA PROD Support
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting approval
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

After some investigating it appears the issues needs to be fixed at the Nile level or whatever pushes the Raw tables to SQL Stg.

 

I need NILE to load the DOS (line_service_to_date) field from the RAW tables to the detail tables. 

Both dates need in the attached example to be fixed by Nile.

The Excel worksheet provided and the query results that I pasted clearly shows that both values are wrong for this particular claim.

 

Note: in the second image, all lines are showing the date range, we are looking specific date of service to each claim line. 

 

Query for attached table.

 
SELECT TOP (1000)
CnlyClaimNum
, LineNum
, LineBeginDOS
, LineEndDOS
FROM
[HealthSpringDataClaimDetail].[dbo].[CnlyClaimsDetail]
WHERE 1=1
ANDCnlyClaimNum='24E162751100'
 
SELECT TOP (1000)
CnlyClaimId
, ClaimLine
, FromDate
, ThroughDate
FROM
[HealthSpringDataRaw].[dbo].[ProfessionalClaims_Load_New]
WHERE 1=1
ANDCnlyClaimId='24E162751100'

 

Reference: RCASA-69496

 Comments

Comment by Price, Ryon [ 21/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 05/May/26 EDT ]

we want to discuss about this ticket get more inputs from the user.

 Description   

Please add the attached table (DX Code Laterality Table) to the Medical Codes Library (MCL). Table is needed for Spotcheck content logic.

 Comments   
Comment by Pineo, Rob [ 21/Apr/26 EDT ]

DX Code Laterality Table.xlsx (1.39 MB)

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Kamsetty, Sravani .

This is a new table and will require a new pipeline.

The DDL for this table is:

CREATE TABLE [dbo].[DxCodeLaterality](
[DiagCode] [varchar](10) NULL,
[Category] [varchar](300) NULL,
[SubCategory] [varchar](300) NULL,
[Description] [varchar](1000) NULL,
[Source] [varchar](500) NULL,
[Laterality] [varchar](20) NULL,
[LastUpdateUser] [varchar](100) NULL,
[SFileDate] [smalldatetime] NULL,
[SFilename] [varchar](100) NULL,
[AutoId] [int] IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL )

The source file has been saved to this location:
Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\DxCodeLaterality\2026-04-01\DX Code Laterality Table.xlsx

Per requestor, the data should be loaded in Append mode.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks.

Mike

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 23/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Deynarian, Michael , Thanks for the info. I started working on the new pipeline and all the required configurations in nile.
Thanks.

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Deynarian, Michael , Data has been loaded to table. Could you verify and confirm please.

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

hello Kamsetty, Sravani .

I have reviewed the table and it looks good. Thank you for getting this done so quickly!

You can close this ticket.

Mike

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Deynarian, Michael , Migrated all the required configurations to production. Closing the ticket.

So, need to modify spooler required to NO.

Files ingestion is failing due to required missing header/trailer records. Please ask for replacement files.

Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support

 Description   

The pipeline dm-bcbs_ma_bcbs_ma-actian_migration_caqh_and_bmc_workflow_CHC_BCBS_MA_ADH_CAQH_Load_NILE has failed during execution.

The error message observed is:
"Exiting Process with failure. Not all mandatory source files are present for Period: 20260331. No sub-stage(s) should run."

However, upon verification, the required source files appear to be available in the expected source location with valid file sizes.

Request:
Kindly investigate why the pipeline is unable to detect the mandatory source files despite their presence in the source path. Please verify file naming, file availability checks, and source configuration for Period 20260331.

Impact:
The pipeline execution is currently blocked due to the mandatory file validation failure, preventing further downstream processing.

 Comments   
Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 21/Apr/26 EDT ]

Filename pattern changed from CAQH file.* to CAQH File.*
Ran the pipeline using override parameters and completed successfully.

Nile Production Support

 Description   

team,

would you please help add EDM_RCA_UHC_CIRRUS@cotiviti.com to uhc cirrus nile ingestion failure email notification

would you please help add EDM_RCA_HCSC@cotiviti.com to HCSC nile ingestion failure email notification

Thanks

 Comments   
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Sravani,
Can we add UHC Cirrus and hcsc-hcsc client feeds with these 4 kinds of emails:

  • Unmatched raw counts
  • Data Drop
  • File came late
  • BMC trigger not running..
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

Unmatched caqh raw data count - 20260404_1 - prod - dm-excellus-actian_migration_caqh.msg prod - Cannot kick off bmc workflow CHC_UHC_COSMOS_PROVIDER_INCREMENTAL_NILE for ingestion period 20260428-1.msg Late Client File Notification - prod - Kaiser - WA - Kaiser-WA-Actian-2.msg Data metric percentage drop hits threshold 202603 - prod - dm-humana-hdap-actian_migration_MEDCLM_RAW_AMBULANCE.msg

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

Sravani,
I attached the example of the emails from other clients..
Hope this helps, thanks!

melly

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 04/May/26 EDT ]

Hi,

As per the current configuration, Nile sends email notifications for BMC success events and threshold percentage drops for certain clients.

If EDM requires failure notifications, there is a parameter (tech_email_dl) that triggers alerts for every event—such as pipeline start, pipeline end, and failures whenever a pipeline run fails as per the schedule.

However, enabling this may lead to excessive notifications and potential confusion between Nile and EDM alerts. This likely needs further discussion. Jani and Krishna are currently looking into it.

Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr

Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

·       Please create MCL table " SpecialtyPharmacyProviders " using the attached Excel table. Updates to be provided by ERT (full replace monthly).

  • Who will be the user of this data?
    • This table supports our next gen solution SpotCheck – We will need this table to refine data output for NPI Pharmacy Providers.
  • Can you give a quick description of the table (what this table is about)?
    • The values within the table depict NPI of pharmacy providers, along with their Provider Organization Name (Legal Business Name), Provider Business Practice Location Address City Name and Provider Business Practice Location Address State Name.
  • Would you share with us what the background is for this request (e.g. How are these tables going to be used the users, reports, processes, etc)?
    • This table will be used to support next gen content for SpotCheck. It will be a central location to house all Specialty Pharmacy Providers.
  • How would this data be provided to the Nile Production Support team going forward?
    • We will set up an monthly cadence with ERT to have them review the policy for any changes and deliver updates. (Replace)
  • Will the files be provided to us or should we access/retrieve the data ourselves?
    • Provided by ERT
  • If the files will be given for us, will the format be consistent from delivery to delivery?
    • Yes
  • What will be the frequency/cadence of these files?
    • Monthly

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 15/Apr/26 EDT ]

We have listed all the BMC pipelines for 20 clients and identified which ones have prechecks configured.

For each pipeline, we opened and validated whether it falls under the module.

If a pipeline is not under the module, we checked how the BMC workflow is being called.
If a pipeline is under the module, we verified whether the prechecks are configured using the latest option.

Any pipelines that are not listed have been identified and shared with Krishna.

[IT-2990408] Request to Schedule dm-aetna-hrp-actian_migration_payment to Avoid Manual Trigger Dependency Created: 15/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 16/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 16/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Raya, Birajan Assignee: Dindo Octaviano
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: dm
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 8 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 15/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Created, tested, published and scheduled Version 1.7
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

For the pipeline dm-aetna-hrp-actian_migration_payment, an update was previously implemented (Version V1.7) to support processing of null-value files.

Currently, every Monday we receive low-size (KB) files, and the pipeline requires manual triggering each time. If the manual trigger is missed, it blocks the subsequent data date from running, causing delays in downstream processing.

Request:
Kindly review the current pipeline configuration and, if all updates are functioning as expected, schedule the pipeline for automatic execution to eliminate the need for manual intervention and prevent future processing delays.

Business Impact:
Manual dependency on triggering increases the risk of missed runs and may block subsequent data date processing. Automating the schedule will ensure timely execution and reduce operational risk.

 Comments   
Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

Created, published and scheduld vrsion 1.7

[IT-2989509] Molina RCCV Data Mapping Created: 14/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 24/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 24/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Arevalo, Anna Assignee: Dindo Octaviano
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: RCCV
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 2 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 17/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Pending Requirements
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: All investigative work on NILE Side is done. DRG mapping change will go on a separate ticket.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: LARGE 8- 24 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

The Molina RCCV data feed is mapped to GDF from the RCA data. Please advise which fields from the RCA data are mapped to CH_ALLOWED_DRG_CODE, CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE, CH_ALLOWED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS, CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS. If there is any logic being applied to the data (removing leading zeros, pulling first n characters, etc), please provide that as well.

 Comments   
Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 15/Apr/26 EDT ]

Here are the mappings:

 

CH_ALLOWED_DRG_CODE     =  CASE WHEN LENGTH(REGEXP_REPLACE(finaldrg,'[.-]',''))>5 THEN '' ELSE LEFT(REGEXP_REPLACE(finaldrg,'[.-]',''),3) END

CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE    = CASE WHEN LENGTH(REGEXP_REPLACE(drg_code,'[.-]',''))>5 THEN '' ELSE LEFT(REGEXP_REPLACE(drg_code,'[.-]',''),3) END

CH_ALLOWED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS    = CASE WHEN LENGTH(REGEXP_REPLACE(finaldrg,'[.-]',''))=4 THEN RIGHT(REGEXP_REPLACE(finaldrg,'[.-]',''),1) ELSE '' END

CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS    = CASE WHEN LENGTH(REGEXP_REPLACE(drg_code,'[.-]',''))=4 THEN RIGHT(REGEXP_REPLACE(drg_code,'[.-]',''),1) ELSE '' END

 

Arevalo, Anna 

 

Comment by Arevalo, Anna [ 15/Apr/26 EDT ]

In addition to the GDF mapping information provided, please also confirm if there is any additional logic or transformation happening from GDF to CCV CDF level for the DRG and SOI fields.

GDF CH_ALLOWED_DRG_CODE / CCV CDF DRG

GDF CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE / CCV CDF SUB_DRG

GDF CH_ALLOWED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS / CCV CDF SEVERITY

GDF CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS / CCV CDF SUB_SEVERITY

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

mappings  for CDF:

 

DRG    = NVL(CH_ALLOWED_DRG_CODE,'')
DRGSubmit    = NVL(CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE,'')
DRGSeverity    = NVL(CH_ALLOWED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS,'')
DRGSubmitType    = CASE WHEN CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE IS NULL THEN '' WHEN LENGTH(CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_CODE)=3 AND CH_SUBMITTED_DRG_SEVERITY_OF_ILLNESS>'' THEN 'APRDRG' ELSE 'MSDRG' END

 

 

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 20/Apr/26 EDT ]

waiting on Anna Arevalo to decide what is needed

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

Arevalo, Anna   agreed to close this and create a separate request to edit and mapped  DRG fields

I have noticed that the file spooled from Nile is different from the files available in FTP( i.e : crossed checked with size and record count manually ) , leading to a discrepancy in the record count. Could you please let us know what could be the possible causes for such an issue?

 Comments   
Comment by KC, Sanjeeb [ 14/Apr/26 EDT ]

Lineage Report 20260331 -1 - prod - dm-bcbs_mi_medadv-actian_migration_claims_pt2-1.msg (79 kB)

Lineage Report 20260331 -1 - prod - dm-bcbs_mi_medadv-actian_migration_claims_pt2.msg (79 kB)

Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

This file came in via attachmate (confirmed with MFT team).
We see occurrence from other clients/files as well: when Attachmate starts transferring the files, eventhough it is still partial, Attachmate makes the files available for transfer.
It doesnt lock/prevent other activities (unlike Axway).

Date Need By: 21/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Production Applications - RCA
GSR Category:
Other

Other

Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: LARGE 8- 24 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hello Nile Production Support,

 For Tufts monthly data load, I noticed for some weekly files pipelines (com_prov, com_group and cob_claims) seem to load files older than previous month. Please see attached report.  These are weekly files and it is loading older files past previous month's files. Can someone please look into this?

It is not causing any issues with downstream processes but want to make sure it does not slow down the process eventually loading weeks of files.

 Comments   
Comment by Trivedi Shukla, Zarna [ 14/Apr/26 EDT ]

Tufts Commercial QC Report_ PreBuild_RecordCounts 2026-04-13.msg (121 kB)

Comment by Trivedi Shukla, Zarna [ 14/Apr/26 EDT ]

Had a meeting with Din and Keaton to look at distinct files and load dates in these tables where the weekly files (com_prov, com_group and cob_claims) are getting appended for the files older than previous month. Checked the control-M workflow and found a job that used to truncate the staging tables before files were loaded, however, this job is apparently set to run as dummy after NILE migration when the previous engineer used to own this audit. Therefore, we would need to set the pipelines for com_prov, com_group and cob_claims files to be set as truncate and append.

[TuftsDataRaw].[dbo].[Provider_Load_Monthly]

[TuftsDataRaw].[dbo].[COB_Claims_Load_Monthly]

[TuftsDataRaw].[dbo].[Member_Group_Load_Monthly]

Comment by Trivedi Shukla, Zarna [ 14/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

Checking with Pacete, John   and Trivedi Shukla, Zarna   to make sue the truncate an insert  is ok to replace incremental logic 

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 20/Apr/26 EDT ]

waiting for the next run

hello, we are getting a new file for BCBS_MN. we have a sample file to start working on the mapping. i have attached it. there are only 3 fields

  • Grp_nbr = Group Number
  • Clnt_nbr = Client Number
  • Clntlgl_nm= Client Legal Name.

We are still awaiting client confirmation on the file naming convention. However, we do know the file is expected monthly, and any members with IDs starting with "TGT" will need to be excluded.

 name(s) BCNtoCotivitiMemberControl202604.txt

File data line count 6028037

Ingested row count 6027999
Action Required Investigate and fix if the issue is due to data quality, data load error, or some other reasons. If issue is minor and can be ignored, in Client Configuration set parameter member_unmatched_raw_count_override_202604 to 1 and proceed with the run

Please investigate and suggest the required action.

[IT-2982952] Request for presbyterian - hrp Monthly improvement Created: 10/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 29/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Kabeya, Joel Assignee: Karakavalasa, SriVarshini
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: RCCV_Internal
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 4 hours, 30 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: image.png    
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting User Information
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Introduced a weekly data validation module to enhance visibility into processed files and those pending processing.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Last month, we encountered an issue with the Presbyterian HRP monthly process. The pipeline ran on March 7th with only one file (rccv file prsby_inpatient_incoming_2r), which turned out to be incomplete and contained only the first week of data.

As a result, we had to reach out to PC and CRS to remove the file, and CRS needed to communicate with the client after submitting a corrected file. This created additional rework and client impact.

To prevent this issue from happening again, I recommend implementing a weekly validation check to ensure all expected files are received before the pipeline runs. This approach has already been successfully implemented in other pipelines that depend on weekly file deliveries.

I will attach a screenshot as an example to demonstrate how those pipelines are configured to wait for all required weekly files before processing.

 Comments   
Comment by Kabeya, Joel [ 10/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 15/Apr/26 EDT ]

I have started working on it and am currently testing in the lower environment. I will keep you posted on the updates.

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hello Kabeya, Joel ,
Upon your request, I have improved the Presbyterian–HRP monthly history institutional claims pipeline, which now provides weekly data validations. Please monitor the pipeline in the next run and let me know if any further changes are required.

Providing the pipeline details below:
pipeline name: rccv-presbyterian-hrp-history_institutional_claims_processed_GDF2_8_CDF2_8

pipeline ID: 2024-01-05-11460210
Version: V1.2

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]

A draft version of the improved pipelines has been deployed to production and requires monitoring for the next run.

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hello Kabeya, Joel ,
Published and scheduled the pipeline in production. Kindly monitor for the next run and let me know if any changes required.




[IT-2982754] Please Create Pipeline dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators_Pipe Created: 10/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 17/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 17/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Deynarian, Michael Assignee: Kamsetty, Sravani
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: rca_actian
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 3 hours
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: DDDL_for_Ing_HCPCSIndicators_Table.txt    
Date Need By: 14/Apr/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Pipeline created and file loaded successfully.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hello Nile Team.

Please create a new pipeline - dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators_Pipe

This will serve as a alternate pipeline to existing pipeline dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators. This new pipeline will have a source file as pipe-delimited with a header record. The file must be loaded in Append mode, since data already exists in the SQL table.

The DDDL for the SQL table is attached.

Here are some notes on the columns:

The BETOS field is not mapped to SQL
The EffectiveDt column is taken from the name of the folder containing the source file and is the first day of the calendar quarter
(i.e., YYYY-01-01
YYYY-04-01
YYYY-07-01
YYYY-10-01)
For example if the folder is Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\Ingenix\HCPCS\2026-04-01 then the EffectiveDt is 2026-04-01.
The EndDt column is the last day of the quarter being loaded
(i.e., if EffectiveDt = YYYY-01-01 then EndDt = YYYY-03-31
if EffectiveDt = YYYY-04-01 then EndDt = YYYY-06-30
if EffectiveDt = YYYY-07-01 then EndDt = YYYY-09-30
if EffectiveDt = YYYY-10-01 then EndDt = YYYY-12-31)

The file name pattern is OPTUM_HCPCS_INDICATORS_YYYY_MM_pipe.txt

 Comments   
Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 10/Apr/26 EDT ]

DDDL_for_Ing_HCPCSIndicators_Table.txt (0.4 kB)

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Kamsetty, Sravani .

I have reviewed the table for this new pipeline and the data looks good.

You can close this ticket.

Thanks for creating this pipeline so quickly!

Mike

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 17/Apr/26 EDT ]

Thanks for the Confirmation Mike.

Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hi Ravi or Din,
We received replacements files for March 2026 today (date folder 20260408).
We can spool everything but these two files:
ASO_CONTRACTS
EXTWEBW

It seems the problem is that these two files have the same hash as the files that we received on 20260330.
I compared the ASO_CONTRACTS between the one that we received on 20260330 and 20260408 and they’re perfect matched.
That’s probably why.

Chao really needs the pipelines going.
Can you help with this spooler issue?
After the spooler issue is resolved, we can have the Nepal team to run the pipeline.

Thank you for your help

melly

 Comments   
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 08/Apr/26 EDT ]

Client feed is hcsc-hcsc

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 09/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Suryani, Melly,
Good morning.

 Description   

+underlined text+Hello Nile team

Operations and NW team found the accountnum for member custom data have an extra leading 0 for 20260228’s file loading,\

according to the findings saved in the excel below, the same accountnum from 202601 does not have the extra leading 0

this issue is causing failing in GWF indicator ,

\\ccaintranet.com\dfs-fld-01\Audits\HCSC\COB\Auditors\Dom\HCSC_COB_SubscriberData_RCASA-78240.xlsx\

Would you please help check if there are any recent change on this hcsc member custom loading pipeline?

pipeline name is : dm-hcsc-hcsc-actian_migration_customer

 Comments   
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 16/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Nile PS Triage team,
I did some reviews on the raw files:
The file in question was related to file EXTTXCUST>
I checked the raw files.
/dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/28-hcsc-hcsc/20260129
/dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/28-hcsc-hcsc/20260301

Since I can't share the screenshot due to PHI, the assignee of this ticket can contact me before he/she can start taking a look.

Thank you

melly

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 20/Apr/26 EDT ]

I have started work on this task. will update the status accordingy.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 22/Apr/26 EDT ]

I have verified the account number following one (0000238000) .They are present in the files listed below, but do not exist in the 20260129 file.

df_Raw0129=spark.read.parquet("/dataingestionprod/unrestricted/28-hcsc-hcsc/AppData/ParquetString/CUSTOMER/2026*/*")

-----------------------------------

ACCOUNT_NUMBER FileId            acc_len

-----------------------------------

00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260408 10    
00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260311 10    
00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260301 10    
00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260407 10    
00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260316 10    
00002XXXXX    EXTTXCUST_20260330 10    

-----------------------------------

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 24/Apr/26 EDT ]


I compared the data with previous months and the upcoming month’s issued folder. The expected account number length is 10, but for the affected month it is 9. This discrepancy is coming from the raw source, and I’ve verified it across all the raw data

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 28/Apr/26 EDT ]

my primary analysis was done, will connect with user and explain about this issue.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 29/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Tang, Chao,
Good morning.
I’ve verified the raw data for the dates 2025-11-29 and 2025-12-29. The maximum account number length is 9 digits. I’ve attached a file for your reference.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 05/May/26 EDT ]

waiting for user confirmation.

Nile Production Support

Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr

Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Good Day.

The record counts for the FWAM BCBS North Dakota CDF are very low for the 02/2026 Nile runs. We are only getting about 25% of our normal CDF records. Below is a record counts for the 01/2026 run (good run) and then the record counts for 02/2026.

01/2026
425849 chhosp_202601.dat
998791 cpprof_202601.dat
709081 crrev_202601.dat
0 d1diag9_202601.dat
592487 d2diag10_202601.dat
0 d3proc9_202601.dat
6806 d4proc10_202601.dat
0 d5diag9_202601.dat
1930270 d6diag10_202601.dat
701827 mbr_202601.dat
61513 prov_202601.dat
61513 provnpi_202601.dat
388556 rxphar_202601.dat

02/2026
96491 chhosp_202602.dat
207950 cpprof_202602.dat
162221 crrev_202602.dat
0 d1diag9_202602.dat
140945 d2diag10_202602.dat
0 d3proc9_202602.dat
1506 d4proc10_202602.dat
0 d5diag9_202602.dat
404037 d6diag10_202602.dat
701827 mbr_202602.dat
61513 prov_202602.dat
61513 provnpi_202602.dat
92308 rxphar_202602.dat
 

This should be prioritzed if at all possible.  The customer is now a month behind with their FWA processing.

 Comments

Can I get a status on this request please?

Comment by Dindo Octaviano [ 08/Apr/26 EDT ]

7 rows or .0002% of the member aree rejected. Crum, David   agreed that we load those  files and send the rejected rows to him and/or CSM.

 

the rejected row will be consistent after each run

all monthly pipelines were run and data is being checked

[IT-2972974] Wellcare PBM trigger file permission issue for new platform process. Created: 06/Apr/26 EDT  Updated: 06/Apr/26 EDT  Resolved: 06/Apr/26 EDT

Status: Cancelled
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Trivedi Shukla, Zarna Assignee: Unassigned
Resolution: Cancelled Votes: 0
Labels: None
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: image-1.png     image.png    
Date Need By: 07/Apr/26 EDT
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Wellcare monthly PBM has two files (Medicare and Medicaid). This month was the first time the monthly PBM process ran on new platform. The Trigger File Handler job moved the Medicare trigger file into archived folder, however, it did not move Medicaid trigger file into archive folder. I reached out to Genesis team and Roy mentioned that the permission on Medicaid file shows as rw-rr- instead of -rwxrwxr-x. That is the reason why Medicaid trigger file did not get moved into archived folder. Can we please fix the permissions on the Medicaid trigger file?  Please see attached screenshots. 

 Comments   
Comment by Trivedi Shukla, Zarna [ 06/Apr/26 EDT ]

Comment by Nair, Lakshmi [ 06/Apr/26 EDT ]

We have checked and couldn't find any issues at our end. And we are using the same code to generate the trigger files for Centene and Medicare as well. As those files are generated fine, we don't think it is the issue with the Nile pipelines that is causing the permission discrepancy.

Comment by Trivedi Shukla, Zarna [ 06/Apr/26 EDT ]

This request needs to be routed to Hadoop team.

Other

Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other

Nile Production Support
Close Notes: all data copied as requested and verified.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hi Team,

Could you please copy all the data for the date given below from the Nile PROD raw path to the Nile QC raw path?

20260322

Nile PROD RAW PATH (Source): /dataingestionprod/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp

Nile QC RAW PATH (Target): /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp

Once copied to QC, the owner should be set to "svcpnilediqcraw" and group should be set to "qcunrestricted" and permissions should be 750.

 Comments   
Comment by Birney, Kevin [ 03/Apr/26 EDT ]

ran the following commands and verified the data matches.  closing ticket.

 

hdfs dfs -ls /dataingestionprod/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322

hdfs dfs -ls /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322

hadoop distcp /dataingestionprod/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322/* /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322/

hdfs dfs -chown -R svcpnilediqcraw /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp
hdfs dfs -chgrp -R qcrestricted /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp
hdfs dfs -chmod -R 750 /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp

hdfs dfs -du /dataingestionprod/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/ | grep 20260322
hdfs dfs -du /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/ | grep 20260322

25474875641  76424626923   /dataingestionprod/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322
[kevin.birney@usapnlhdpe01 ~] <PROD>$ hdfs dfs -du /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/ | grep 20260322
25481438669  76444316007   /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260322

Close Notes: hmsmd_history_institutional_incoming_1_20260401 was processed successfully by NILE upon receipt of client file, 4/1/2026. The file failed on PC side for reasons mentioned in the description. Triage team confirmed we required a replacement file with the errors corrected to successfully send this data through PC. We notified the client of the need for the replacement file promptly on 4/1/2026. We sent follow up on 4/12 and 4/21 and no responses were received from the client indicating whether or not they would be sending a replacement file.

 Description   

Hi Team,
Could you please copy the below mentioned files from the Nile QC location to the Nile DEV location?
for the February month files.

/dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260222
/dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260215
/dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260208
/dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260201

Nile QC RAW PATH (Source): /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/
Nile Dev RAW PATH (Target): /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp

Once copied to QC folder, the owner should be set to "svcpnilediqcraw" and group should be set to "qcrestricted" and permissions should be 75

 Comments   
Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 01/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Shivalingu, Naveen

files copied

please veify

hdfs dfs -cp /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260222 /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/
hdfs dfs -cp /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260215 /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/

hdfs dfs -cp /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260208 /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/
hdfs dfs -cp /dataingestionqc/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/20260201 /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp/

hdfs dfs -chown -R svcpniledidevraw /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp
hdfs dfs -chgrp -R devrestricted /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp
hdfs dfs -chmod -R 750 /dataingestiondev/raw/restricted/19-uhc-csp

Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: Rca_Internal

GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting User Information
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: The file was reprocessed due to a missing layout, but the issue persisted. After further investigation, the user modified the file and resubmitted the pipeline, which then completed successfully without any errors.

The user has received the expected results. Awaiting confirmation from the user before proceeding to close the ticket.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Hello Nile Team.

Please run the below pipeline with the indicated source file to update the ICD10PcsOR table in the GlobalHCCnlyMedicalCodesLibrary database.

Pipeline: dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ICD10PcsOR
Source file location: Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\ICD10OR\2026-04-01\ICD10PcsOR_2026_April.xlsx

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks.

Mike

 Comments   
Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 07/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hello Kantimahanti, Ravisankar .

There is an issue with the [GlobalHCCnlyMedicalCodesLibrary].[dbo].ICD10PcsOR table.

There are 79,477 in the source file, but there are 387,246 rows in the SQL table. There should be 79,477 rows in the SQL table.

Can you please check and re-load the table?

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks.

Mike

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 08/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Deynarian, Michael,

I have to respooled the new file and resubmitted the pipeline. Will keep you posted on the progress.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 09/Apr/26 EDT ]

waiting for user confirmation.

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 10/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hi Kantimahanti, Ravisankar .

I just received the Lineage Report for ICD10PcsOR and checked the table.  the issue still persists - it loaded 387,246 records but the source file contains 79,477 rows.

I have created a copy of the ICD10PcsOR_2026_April.xlsx file by copying the 79,477 rows from that file into a new file -  ICD10PcsOR_2026_April_Resaved.xlsx.  
it is located here: Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\ICD10OR\2026-04-01\ICD10PcsOR_2026_April_Resaved.xlsx.

Can you please try loading that file?

If this does not work, I think you may need to get some assistance on your end to see why this pipeline is not working.

We need to have this loaded properly by Wednesday of next week (Apr 15).

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 10/Apr/26 EDT ]

Hello Kantimahanti, Ravisankar

The new file worked! There are now 79,477 rows in the source file and 79,477 in the ICD10PcsOR SQL table!

Thank you for your help and patience in getting this done.

You can close the ticket.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 13/Apr/26 EDT ]

The file was reprocessed due to a missing layout, but the issue persisted. After further investigation, the user modified the file and resubmitted the pipeline, which then completed successfully without any errors.

The user has received the expected results. Awaiting confirmation from the user before proceeding to close the ticket.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 13/Apr/26 EDT ]

The file was reprocessed due to a missing layout, but the issue persisted. After further investigation, the user modified the file and resubmitted the pipeline, which then completed successfully without any errors.

The user has received the expected results. Awaiting confirmation from the user before proceeding to close the ticket.

 Description   

Please update MCL table "McdDmeCappedRentalCodes" and “DmeZipcodeClassification” using the attached Excel table. Updates to be provided by ERT (full replace quarterly).

  • Who will be the user of this data?
    • This table supports our next gen solution SpotCheck – We will need this table to refine data output for ‘McdDmeCappedRentalCodes’ Billing.
  • Can you give a quick description of the table (what this table is about)?
    • The values within the table depict specific HCPCS and a specific Category they fall into, as well as Area Classification along with their Zip Codes for two States.
  • Would you share with us what the background is for this request (e.g. How are these tables going to be used the users, reports, processes, etc)?
    • This table will be used to support next gen content for SpotCheck. It will be a central location to house all ‘McdDmeCappedRentalCodes’ Billing.
  • How would this data be provided to the Nile Production Support team going forward?
    • We will set up a quarterly cadence with ERT to have them review the policy for any changes and deliver updates. (Replace)
  • Will the files be provided to us or should we access/retrieve the data ourselves?
    • Provided by ERT
  • If the files will be given for us, will the format be consistent from delivery to delivery?
    • Yes
  • What will be the frequency/cadence of these files?
    • Quarterly
